article_meta.title: News Law & the Courts Black Lives Matter Director Spent Millions in Donations on Homes, Shopping, Vacations, Indictment

reasoning: The summary largely avoids the source’s loaded framing, uses attribution (e.g., “indicted,” “allege,” “indictment claims”), and maintains a neutral tone. One mildly interpretive phrase (“luxury purchases”) appears, and the broader-context line is framed as the article’s characterization rather than fact. Minor omissions of counterpoints (e.g., Cullors’s denial) are present but not central.

custom_reasoning.spin: Language is mostly neutral with one mild interpretive term (“luxury purchases”).
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All key claims are attributed to the indictment or article context; DOJ probe referenced as reported.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative elements are either avoided or explicitly attributed to the article/indictment.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming wording beyond factual allegations.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives or intentions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Slight emphasis on allegations and broader negative context; limited inclusion of mitigating context.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Does not mention Cullors’s explicit denial noted in the article; otherwise captures main points.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone mirrors the article’s allegations without amplification.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preference for elite vs. grassroots sources; relies on indictment and reported facts.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source; uses careful attribution and avoids loaded phrasing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Covers prosecutors/indictment and organizational records but omits explicit mention of denials; ‘sources_represented’ could better reflect perspectives (e.g., prosecutors, fiscal sponsor, DOJ).
